content.full_text:
August 25, 1992

Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

Re: Conflict of Interest Advice Letter

Dear John:

Thank you for your letter of July 23, 1992 (A-92-366)
regarding Planning Commissioner Erv Suess. Based on your
letter, I advised Mr. Suess to abstain from participation in
the rezoning and he has done so.

One of my Councilmembers, Gene Wolfe, also lives in
Rossmoor, but in a condominium not a housing cooperative. Mr.
Wolfe has authorized me to seek your advice involving his
participation in the same rezoning issue as the previous
letter; but as you will see, Mr. Wolfe's ownership situation is
quite different.

As we discussed earlier, Rossmoor is a private gated
community within the City of Walnut Creek. Numerous separate
and legally distinct condominium projects exist within the
boundaries of Rossmoor. Unlike the housing cooperative
ownership structure set forth in the Suess letter, each of the
condominium projects include common areas which are owned
solely by the owners of the units within the particular
project. A number of nonprofit corporations ("Mutuals") manage
the common areas for the owners, but the Mutuals do not
themselves own the common areas. Approximately 8,348 people
live in 6,228 units within Rossmoor, of which 2,860 are
condominium units.

Another nonprofit corporation, the Golden Rain Foundation,
owns the roads, water lines, sewer lines, recreational
facilities, meeting rooms, a golf course, maintenance
facilities and a health services building. This property does
not include any of the common areas of condominium projects
themselves, but instead includes the basic infrastructure
inside Rossmoor which is similar to what the City, the water
district and the sewer district own outside of Rossmoor. The

P.O. Box 8039, 1666 North Main Street, Walnut Creek, California 94596 (415) 943-5800

Mr. John Wallace
August 25, 1992
Page 2


property is owned in trust for the owners within Rossmoor, but
the owners do not own this property themselves.

Mr. Wolfe owns a condominium in a condominium project
known as Lot 2 of Project 50. There are 34 units in Lot 2. As
an owner of one of the condominium units, Mr. Wolfe also owns
1/34th of the common area within Lot 2. Mr. Wolfe owns no
interest and has no financial responsibility for any other
condominium project or property within Rossmoor.

Third Walnut Creek Mutual is a nonprofit corporation which
manages the property of 32 separate condominium projects, one
of which is Lot 2 of Project 50. Third Walnut Creek Mutual
itself does not own any property. Third Walnut Creek Mutual
contracts with another organization for maintenance of the
condominium projects.

The issue of whether to rezone the "Del Valle site" will
soon be before the City Council. The location of this site and
the other relevant properties are indicated on the attached
map. The Del Valle site is outlined in orange. The
condominium project (Lot 2 of Project 50) which includes Mr.
Wolfe's condominium unit is a portion of the area designated as
"M50" in the lower right-hand corner of the map. This project
is more than 8,500 feet from the Del Valle site. The projects
whose property is managed by Third Walnut Creek Mutual are
outlined in yellow. The closest property to the Del Valle site
managed by Third Walnut Creek Mutual is condominium project 36,
which is less than 300 feet from the Del Valle site. Again,
neither Third Walnut Creek Mutual nor Mr. Wolfe own any
property in project 36. Golden Rain Foundation owns, among
other things, streets within the Del Valle site.

After reviewing cases and advice letters your office
relied on in your July 23, 1992, letter, it would appear that
Mr. Wolfe only has a material financial interest in his
condominium unit and the common property within his condominium
project. Accordingly, his situation would fall under Section
18702.3(b) of the regulations. I would appreciate it if you
could confirm this analysis based on the facts of this letter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
THOMAS HAAS
City Attorney

cc: Councilmember Wolfe

MUTUAL CORPORATIONS

FIRST WALNUT CREEK MUTUAL
PROJECTS: 1, 3-7

SECOND WALNUT CREEK MUTUAL
PROJECTS: 2, 10-17

THIRD WALNUT CREEK MUTUAL
PROJECTS: 9, 18-20, 24-27,
32-47, 49-55 & 64

FOURTH WALNUT CREEK MUTUAL
PROJECTS: 8, 21-23, 28-31,
48, 56 & 58

COMMUNITY FACILITIES

BG    Buckeye Grove
DV    Del Valle Clubhouse
EFC   Entry Control Facility
GW    Gateway Clubhouse
H     Hillside Clubhouse
HS    Health Services Building
JD    Junior Dollar Clubhouse
MS    Maintenance Services Center
P7    Parcel Seven Property
RVP   R V Parking
SA    Stables Area
SD    Senior Dollar Clubhouse

STREETS

AS    Avenida Sevilla
CC    Cactus Court
CW    Canyonwood Court
EW    Edgewater Court
FF    Fairtown Court
GR    Golden Rain Road
LL    Leisure Lane
LS    Lakeshire Drive
OD    Oakmont Drive
OW    Oakmont Way
PK    Pine Knoll
PT    Ptermigan Drive
RL    Rockledge Lane
RP    Rossmoor Parkway
RS    Running Springs Road
RV    Rockview Drive
SD    Stanley Dolor Drive
SI    Saklan Indian Drive
SK    Skycrest Drive
SW    Singingwood Court
TC    Terra California Drive
TCK   Ice Creek Drive
TG    Terra Granada Drive
TV    Tice Valley Blvd
WH    Whitehall Court

ROSSMOOR MUTUAL CORPORATIONS

AND COMMUNITY FACILITIES

[Scale bar showing 0   500 1000]

4/6/90
